items.title: Middle East crisis live: Trump claims Iran war will be over ‘very soon’ but Tehran says it will determine when
items.standfirst: US president says war is ‘very complete’ and threatens worse strikes if passage of oil via strait of Hormuz is blocked; IRGC says it will not let out ‘one litre of oil’Oil prices drop sharply after Trump moves to reassure marketsTrump says Iran war is ‘very complete, pretty much’ as economic toll risesHow have you been affected by the latest Middle East events?Investor hopes for a swift resolution to the Middle East conflict propelled Australian shares higher today, with the benchmark S&P/ASX 200 finishing the day up 1.1% and recovering about $35bn in value after yesterday’s $90bn plunge.Oil prices surged to a four-year high early in the week before coming back down below $US90 a barrel after Donald Trump suggested the Iran conflict would end soon, sending global stock markets higher. Continue reading...
items.content: US president says war is ‘very complete’ and threatens worse strikes if passage of oil via strait of Hormuz is blocked; IRGC says it will not let out ‘one litre of oil’Oil prices drop sharply after Trump moves to reassure marketsTrump says Iran war is ‘very complete, pretty much’ as economic toll risesHow have you been affected by the latest Middle East events?Investor hopes for a swift resolution to the Middle East conflict propelled Australian shares higher today, with the benchmark S&P/ASX 200 finishing the day up 1.1% and recovering about $35bn in value after yesterday’s $90bn plunge.Oil prices surged to a four-year high early in the week before coming back down below $US90 a barrel after Donald Trump suggested the Iran conflict would end soon, sending global stock markets higher. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump gives mixed messages about when the war with Iran will end
items.standfirst: In a phone call with CBS News Monday, Trump said "the war is very complete." But at a separate event with Republican lawmakers, he said the U.S. still needed to achieve "ultimate victory."
items.content: In a phone call with CBS News Monday, Trump said "the war is very complete." But at a separate event with Republican lawmakers, he said the U.S. still needed to achieve "ultimate victory."(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)

items.title: Energy prices ease and stocks recover on hopes Trump will end war soon
items.standfirst: Oil prices have fallen and stock markets are clawing back some lost ground after Donald Trump raised hopes that Iran war disruption to the global economy would soon be over.
items.content: Oil prices have fallen and stock markets are clawing back some lost ground after Donald Trump raised hopes that Iran war disruption to the global economy would soon be over.

items.title: Details on targets hit in Iran, across Middle East during first 10 days of war
items.standfirst: On Monday, President Trump told CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang that the war in Iran "is very complete," but he did not provide a timeline for the end of the conflict. Lindsey Reiser takes a look at the war's first 10 days and some of the strikes that have been carried out across the Middle East.
items.content: On Monday, President Trump told CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang that the war in Iran "is very complete," but he did not provide a timeline for the end of the conflict. Lindsey Reiser takes a look at the war's first 10 days and some of the strikes that have been carried out across the Middle East.

items.title: Trump tells CBS News "the war is very complete"
items.standfirst: President Trump told CBS News the U.S. war with Iran is "very complete," and said the U.S. "could do a lot" about the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: President Trump told CBS News the U.S. war with Iran is "very complete," and said the U.S. "could do a lot" about the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Why has the Iran war sparked fears of stagflation for the global economy?
items.standfirst: Conflict in the Middle East risks knocking growth worldwide and boosting prices, economists warn, amid global market turbulenceMiddle East crisis – live updatesOil prices surged on Monday, triggering a stark sell-off across some of the world’s leading stock markets amid growing concern that the US-Israel war on Iran could set the stage for a global economic shock.While they fell back on Tuesday after Donald Trump suggested the Middle East conflict could end “very soon”, oil continues to trade at high levels. Continue reading...
items.content: Conflict in the Middle East risks knocking growth worldwide and boosting prices, economists warn, amid global market turbulenceMiddle East crisis – live updatesOil prices surged on Monday, triggering a stark sell-off across some of the world’s leading stock markets amid growing concern that the US-Israel war on Iran could set the stage for a global economic shock.While they fell back on Tuesday after Donald Trump suggested the Middle East conflict could end “very soon”, oil continues to trade at high levels. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump says Iran war could end 'soon' as Middle East conflict rattles markets
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Monday suggested the war with Iran could end soon but gave no clear timeline for halting attacks that have rattled the Middle East and global markets. Stocks rose after he told CBS the US-Israeli assault was “very complete,” though later remarks offered mixed signals.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Monday suggested the war with Iran could end soon but gave no clear timeline for halting attacks that have rattled the Middle East and global markets. Stocks rose after he told CBS the US-Israeli assault was “very complete,” though later remarks offered mixed signals.

items.title: US stock markets close on high after Iran war drove oil prices above $100 a barrel
items.standfirst: Markets settled after Trump claimed US-Israel war with Iran is ‘very complete’, bringing oil prices down to $85 a barrelHow high could oil prices go – and what might the global economic fallout be?US stock markets closed on a high after oil prices swung wildly on Monday, reaching a four-year high in the morning that rattled Asian and European markets before settling down once Donald Trump said the US-Israel war with Iran is “very complete”.After surging past $100 a barrel on Monday morning, oil prices came down to $85 a barrel by the time that US stock markets closed in the afternoon. US stocks leaped at a report from a CBS News reporter that Trump thinks “the war is very complete, pretty much” because “they have no navy, no communications, they’ve got no air force”. Continue reading...
items.content: Markets settled after Trump claimed US-Israel war with Iran is ‘very complete’, bringing oil prices down to $85 a barrelHow high could oil prices go – and what might the global economic fallout be?US stock markets closed on a high after oil prices swung wildly on Monday, reaching a four-year high in the morning that rattled Asian and European markets before settling down once Donald Trump said the US-Israel war with Iran is “very complete”.After surging past $100 a barrel on Monday morning, oil prices came down to $85 a barrel by the time that US stock markets closed in the afternoon. US stocks leaped at a report from a CBS News reporter that Trump thinks “the war is very complete, pretty much” because “they have no navy, no communications, they’ve got no air force”. Continue reading...

items.title: CBS News’ Scott MacFarlane leaves network: ‘I look forward to some independence’
items.standfirst: Justice correspondent most known for his January 6 Capitol riot coverage, is latest to quit Bari Weiss-led networkSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxScott MacFarlane, the CBS News justice correspondent most known for his extensive coverage of the January 6 Capitol insurrection, surprised colleagues on Monday morning by announcing his departure from the network, which he joined in late 2021.MacFarlane is only the latest departure from the Bari Weiss-led network, though he said in a memo to colleagues – also posted on LinkedIn – that he personally made the decision to leave. Continue reading...
items.content: Justice correspondent most known for his January 6 Capitol riot coverage, is latest to quit Bari Weiss-led networkSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxScott MacFarlane, the CBS News justice correspondent most known for his extensive coverage of the January 6 Capitol insurrection, surprised colleagues on Monday morning by announcing his departure from the network, which he joined in late 2021.MacFarlane is only the latest departure from the Bari Weiss-led network, though he said in a memo to colleagues – also posted on LinkedIn – that he personally made the decision to leave. Continue reading...

items.title: Live Nation reaches surprise settlement with justice department in antitrust case
items.standfirst: Live Nation will pay $280m to states in lawsuit, and Ticketmaster will open parts of platform to rival companiesSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxLive Nation, which owns Ticketmaster, has reached a surprise settlement with the Department of Justice in its antitrust case just one week after the trial began.Under the agreement, Live Nation will create a $280m settlement fund for states that participated in the lawsuit and Ticketmaster will be required to open parts of its platform to rival ticketing companies, Live Nation announced Monday. Continue reading...
items.content: Live Nation will pay $280m to states in lawsuit, and Ticketmaster will open parts of platform to rival companiesSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxLive Nation, which owns Ticketmaster, has reached a surprise settlement with the Department of Justice in its antitrust case just one week after the trial began.Under the agreement, Live Nation will create a $280m settlement fund for states that participated in the lawsuit and Ticketmaster will be required to open parts of its platform to rival ticketing companies, Live Nation announced Monday. Continue reading...

items.content: A woman was arrested on Sunday for firing multiple shots at the Beverly Hills home of Rihanna, Los Angeles Police Department officials say.

bullet_summary: The case in brief A federal jury found three brothers guilty after a lengthy trial that examined allegations they used wealth and influence to exploit dozens of women

items.title: What led to the Alexander brothers' conviction? #world
items.standfirst: The case in brief A federal jury found three brothers guilty after a lengthy trial that examined allegations they used wealth and influence to exploit dozens of women. items.title: High-profile US real estate brothers convicted in sex trafficking case
items.standfirst: Prosecutors accused the brothers of using their wealth to lure women to parties and luxury homes, then taking advantage of them.

items.title: Why are TSA security lines over three hours? #travel
items.standfirst: The partial government shutdown has tightened staffing at the Transportation Security Administration, causing notable shortages at security checkpoints nationwide. Many agents are federal employees who, during the shutdown, missed at least one scheduled paycheck; that absence triggered higher…
items.content: The partial government shutdown has tightened staffing at the Transportation Security Administration, causing notable shortages at security checkpoints nationwide. items.title: Long security lines at U.S. airports as DHS funding affects TSA staffing
items.standfirst: Wait times to get through security hit two hours in New Orleans and over three hours in Houston as TSA staffing took a hit amid the partial government shutdown.
items.content: Wait times to get through security hit two hours in New Orleans and over three hours in Houston as TSA staffing took a hit amid the partial government shutdown.

items.title: Why are TSA lines stretching past three hours? #travel
items.standfirst: The short explanation: staffing and travel demand Security checkpoints at many U.S. airports are seeing exceptionally long waits because staffing levels at the Transportation Security Administration have been squeezed just as passenger volumes rise. items.title: Yann LeCun Raises $1 Billion to Build AI That Understands the Physical World
items.standfirst: Meta’s former chief AI scientist has long argued that human-level AI will come from mastering the physical world, not language. His new startup, AMI, aims to prove it.
items.content: Meta’s former chief AI scientist has long argued that human-level AI will come from mastering the physical world, not language. His new startup, AMI, aims to prove it.
